name: Dean Martin
description: American singer, actor, comedian and film producer (1917–1995)
abstract: Dean Martin (born Dino Paul Crocetti; June 7, 1917 – December 25, 1995) was an American singer, actor, comedian and television host. Nicknamed the "King of Cool", he is regarded as one of the most popular entertainers of the 20th century.

concerts.label: Tribute to Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr.

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Mead
about_quotes.evidence: Dean Martin is mentioned by the interviewer as an example of a mid-fifties hit artist; the interviewee does not respond with any personal connection or interaction.
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Mead interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, June 1, 2006

about_quotes.speaker: Tom McIntosh
about_quotes.evidence: I discovered that Dean Martin and Frank Sinatra all said that it was the leader of the Mills Brothers that made them want to sing.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom McIntosh — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jerry Gordon
about_quotes.evidence: you could hear the British Invasion, Motown, Dean Martin, and Slim Harpo, all within the same hour
about_quotes.doc_title: Jerry Gordon interviewed by Monk Rowe and Christian Goodwillie, via Zoom, June 30, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Big Chielf Moore
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee (Big Chief Moore) discusses Frank Sinatra recognizing him. Dean Martin is mentioned by the interviewer as part of a group ('The Clan') that was present, but the interviewee does not mention any direct interaction, relationship, or personal connection with Dean Martin.

about_quotes.speaker: Luther Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: you worked with Red Skelton, Dean Martin... The Dean Martin Show... I don't mean to denigrate them... you're a professional arranger, composer... you do it and you hope you get paid for it
about_quotes.doc_title: Luther Henderson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jon Hendricks
about_quotes.evidence: Dean Martin, Jerry Lewis; all the comedians – Don Rickles, Red Foxx, Fatjack Leonard; all the singers – Peggy Lee, Pearl Bailey, Lena Horne. Everybody in show business loved us.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon Hendricks — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
